title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement
summary: Delivery slows while storage cost grows, teams rerun failures without diagnosis, logs expose identifiers, exceptions never expire, and no one can reconstruct which change reached which users. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci policy exception continuous improvement increment in delivery operations and governance defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.
Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:

  setup-node: 7.0.0

  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement. A defensible solution applies Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery operations and governance defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement — Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-4
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-observability-incidents-governance-4
    artifact: delivery-operations.yaml
    decision: worked ci observability incidents governance 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      metrics: queue-critical-path-failure-class-recovery
      cardinality: bounded
      privacy: actor-and-input-minimized
      retention: risk-cost-policy
      incident: contain-preserve-restore-notify
      first-causal-failure: reconstructed
      credential-response: revoke-and-rotate
      artifact-recovery: prior-verified-digest
      exception: owner-reason-expiry
      drill: quarterly
      improvement: outcome-reviewed
    verify: reconstruct one delivery incident from event workflow runner dependency artifact deployment telemetry audit containment and recovery evidence
    failure: Reject unbounded metric labels, forever retention, blind reruns, workflow cancellation as containment, or non-expiring central policy exceptions.
    repair: Measure useful flow safely, retain by need, diagnose first cause, contain authority and mutations, restore verified artifacts, expire exceptions, and drill.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
